title: The 47 State Legal AR: New CMMG BR4 Dissent First Shots
body: I didn't even know threaded barrels were outlawed in 3 states until just now. That's insane. Reduced flash (flash hider) = illegal, reduced deafening noise (suppressor) = illegal, reduced recoil (comp) = illegal. Wow

title: The 47 State Legal AR: New CMMG BR4 Dissent First Shots

title: The 47 State Legal AR: New CMMG BR4 Dissent First Shots
body:
honestly the profile of it actually looks pretty cool, it breaks the mold of all the AR copycats while not being some weird abomination that is trying to not be a pistol grip while also refusing to be a traditional rife stock.

I'm waiting for somebody to make an American SKS with a fixed mag to get around the BS, and with a stripper clip guide.
author: @NYG5

title: The 47 State Legal AR: New CMMG BR4 Dissent First Shots
body: I just found 3 on the wall at 2A Zone in Upland, CA. They're selling at MSRP. It's still out of my price range, but if anyone's in the area I'd find it hilarious if I show up to pick up my lower for my next build and they say they sold them in a day. I think I will be saving for one though. After looking it over, it's a rifle I wouldn't mind having even in a free state. You could feel the quality, and for a featureless rifle, it felt incredibly ergonomic. It's even better than the standing featureless grip champion, the juggernaut grip.

title: Day in the Life | Electrician Apprentice #shorts #constructionwork #apprentice #buildingmindgrasp
body: 45 year retired electrician here, This is one of the best career paths you could have taken in these times. After you become proficient in installations, try to steer toward electrical maintenance and trouble shooting. It's easier on the bones and very satisfying. You'll be a hero to somebody every day.

title: Pass Your Life and Health Insurance Exam on the First Try (Passed in 4 days)
body: Testing Tomorrow afternoon! Pray for me please yall. Been studying for 2 weeks 10 hrs a day. Best tip is (like adam said) take as many practice tests and end of chapter exams as possible. I wrote down the answers I got wrong from each test and FOCUSED on them. Also listening to the lectures helps the information get into you subconscious mind. Last tip, go with your first answer because this is your subconscious mind giving you the right answer! Your conscious mind will second guess and try and make you pick the second answer. Peace and Love and good luck future Agents <3
author: @GhostWarior316

title: Pass Your Life and Health Insurance Exam on the First Try (Passed in 4 days)
body: I passed my test yesterday on the first try. I used Xcel to learn, after completing the prelicensing course there is a cram course with cliff notes available and it has loads of review questions per chapter. The cliff notes do a great job on focusing what's actually on the test vs reading all the useless crap the prelicensing course had you read 😅. I basically brute forced my way thru the prelicensing without reading much and using Quizlet to help me pass the chapter reviews. The cliff notes/review+ memorizing key words+ Quizlet is what helped me the most
author: @TheNOTORIOUSB.E.G

body: I'm going to try this because I'm a hands on learner. Reading and sitting still just isn't my way of learning. Thank you!

title: Pass Your Life and Health Insurance Exam on the First Try (Passed in 4 days)
body:
He is correct. Some new agents passed with a high score but we're not self motivated to move forward with the opportunity.
It's getting to a mentor who wants to spend their time to give the best training on not only products but rapport building and keeping good clients.

Keeping that relationship is vital as clients needs change and to see if referrals are possible.

I've had my Life, Annuity and Health for years in California.
I'm in Texas now so I have to start all over as a new Lone Star resident.

title: Unboxing Delaware: What It's Like Living In Delaware
body: My husband was from Delaware and though born in wilmington he grew up in Lewes (glad you pronounced it correctly). We shopped there when we lived in Maryland.  He loved the slower, lower lifestyle.  You didn't mention pumpkin chucking and the apple scrapple festival.  In Dover Bylers grocery store is a tourist attraction because of the Amish.
author: @carolyndavis6657

title: Unboxing Delaware: What It's Like Living In Delaware
body: Growing up in the suburbs of Philadelphia, if my parents were throwing a dinner or bridge party, I knew that meant a shopping trip to Delaware. Much cheaper booze than the PA state stores.
author: @eurodoc6343

title: Unboxing Delaware: What It's Like Living In Delaware
body: Traffic always been bad in Delaware. I grew up in Delaware. Because you got people from NJ and new York and Maryland and PA.
author: @missleo2003

title: Unboxing Delaware: What It's Like Living In Delaware
body: Keep up the interviews of locals Nick - Really adds a valuable dimension to your efforts...  Work that has had a steady rate of marked improvement over the years.  Nice work!
author: @JF-xq6fr

title: Unboxing Delaware: What It's Like Living In Delaware
body: Lived in Delaware my whole life. Went to Philly for about 8 years and came right back to Wilmington. To outsiders it's boring, but you have to be down to know where the fun is. Wilmington is more carved up than you explained. I like how you can bounce from different scenery and cultures in literally a few minutes. Crime is crazy in Wilmington, but if you're not in the game you're relatively safe.
author: @kryptonite9804

title: Unboxing Delaware: What It's Like Living In Delaware
body: I grew up going to Bethany beach. My grandparents had a beach house there. Many fond memories of that place.
author: @charjl96

title: Unboxing Delaware: What It's Like Living In Delaware
body: Delaware born and raised, the place  I grew up in is/was an Italian-American neighborhood located next to the historic area of New Castle.
author: @BAxeWarrior

title: Unboxing Delaware: What It's Like Living In Delaware
body: I love this state. So many secret places to hang out, and each county is basically its own jig, and when we all come together we can do something beautiful.

body: Delaware. The first state in the union, but the last state anyone really thinks about. Small and relatively peaceful. Never been there, but it would be nice!

title: Unboxing Delaware: What It's Like Living In Delaware
body: Jeremy mentioned a part of Wilmington to avoid is "Brownville".  The area is actually called Browntown.  It gets its name from Doctor Brown who owned a farm [named the Anchorage] there in the mid-1800's. It is believed that Underground Railroad activity occurred there because of its proximity to the Christina & Delaware Rivers.
author: @johnkojro8709

title: Unboxing Delaware: What It's Like Living In Delaware
body: The C&D canal is north of Middletown, and both are entirely contained in New Castle County. It is possible to walk across the state with only 13 miles, just by going along the canal.
author: @hypnovia

title: College Life Presents: University of Delaware
body: Please please please do University of Texas at Austin, Texas Tech, or University of Texas at El Paso. It would really help and I would really appreciate it. Also love the videos.
author: @juliomadrid6928

title: College Life Presents: University of Delaware
body: Loved this video!!! Finally found a great college tour channel with info that we ACTUALLY want to know! Would love to see Villanova University, Uconn, Quinnipiac, Marist College!!
author: @giannacoluccio890

title: College Life Presents: University of Delaware
body: Please go to WASHU in St. Louis next time. There is literally no quality video content available about the university!
author: @samarthkalraVEVO

title: College Life Presents: University of Delaware
body: + College Life Presents Any chance you could do University of Maryland College Park or any other schools close to Maryland and DC! I would really appreciate it, as I am applying to transfer soon and would like to learn more about the schools! Thanks!
author: @AISK9

title: College Life Presents: University of Delaware
body: XD Grottos isn't really a bar. It's a pizza place. It's like calling Applebee's a bar. It's still awesome though.
author: @weldin
